data.body:
## Gap Analysis Amendment (from Step 6)

### Gap: _phase_version_bump double-bump on --resume

If _phase_version_bump is interrupted after modifying the version file but before _state_mark_complete is called, --resume will re-run the phase and call bump-version.sh again, producing a double increment (e.g., 0.1.1 → 0.1.2 instead of staying at 0.1.1).

### Required fix

_phase_version_bump must be idempotent with respect to --resume. Before calling bump-version.sh, check whether the version file was already modified in the current merge commit (compared to its pre-merge state). If it was already bumped, skip the bump and proceed to mark_complete.

Implementation approach:
  # Read version from origin/main (pre-merge baseline)
  _pre_merge_version=
  # Read current version from working tree
  _current_version=
  if [[ -n "_pre_merge_version" && "_current_version" != "_pre_merge_version" ]]; then
    echo 'INFO: Version already bumped in this merge (resume skip).'
    _state_mark_complete version_bump
    return 0
  fi

### Additional acceptance criterion to add

- [ ] _phase_version_bump is idempotent: if version file was already bumped in the current merge commit, calling the phase again skips the bump and exits 0
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-merge-to-main-version-bump.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'test_version_bump_phase_idempotent_on_resume.*PASS'
